data.title: Germany is said to push for breakthrough on stalled European fighter-jet program
data.description:
Germany, France and Spain are preparing for a new round of talks this week aimed at reviving Europe’s long-delayed next-generation fighter initiative, a project expected to cost up to €100 billion ($117 billion) and viewed as central to strengthening the continent’s defense posture amid the ongoing war in Ukraine, Reuters reported Thursday.

The latest effort comes as political leaders face increasing pressure to rescue the Future Combat Air System (FCAS) program, known as SCAF in France, which was launched more than eight years ago but has since become bogged down by disputes between European aerospace champions.

German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius will host his French and Spanish counterparts, Catherine Vautrin and Margarita Robles, in Berlin on Thursday, with the fate of FCAS among the key items up for discussion. A spokesperson for Germany’s Defense Ministry confirmed to Reuters that major procurement issues would be on the agenda, though did not explicitly name the fighter-jet effort.

At the heart of the impasse is a long-running disagreement between Dassault Aviation (DUAVF) and Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) over how responsibilities for development and manufacturing should be allocated. The deadlock has spilled over into national politics: Chancellor Friedrich Merz and French President Emmanuel Macron are expected to take up the matter at a bilateral meeting next week.

Some German lawmakers have floated a new approach that would emphasize shared digital-network capabilities, often referred to as the program’s “Combat Cloud,” and unmanned platforms, potentially allowing each country to proceed with its own fighter aircraft while still collaborating on core technologies.

Labor threats

Tensions escalated further on Wednesday when IG Metall, Germany’s influential industrial union, warned it would cease cooperation on FCAS altogether if Dassault (DUAVF) remained a central participant. In a letter to Pistorius and Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil, a senior union official said Dassault’s insistence on exclusive leadership had eroded trust.

France’s metalworking industry association, headed by Dassault CEO Eric Trappier, pushed back sharply, arguing that sidelining French industry was unacceptable. Trappier has previously said the project requires clearer lines of authority as the partners prepare for a second-phase demonstrator aircraft.

Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY) chief executive Guillaume Faury, speaking on France Inter radio, said he remained optimistic a way forward would be found, though he acknowledged that the companies have yet to settle on a cooperation model.

EU leaders are scheduled to meet in Brussels from December 17 to 19. Merz has indicated he wants a definitive decision on whether FCAS moves ahead before the end of the year.

data.title: Manchester United posts revenue miss, wider loss in Q1
data.description:
Shares of Manchester United (MANU) are down on Thursday after the English football club reported a wider loss and a lower-than-expected revenue in its first quarter of fiscal 2026.

MANU stock fell nearly 4% in early premarket trading but pared those early losses and is trading now down 1.5% at $15.16 in the U.S.

Revenue was weaker in all three operating segments; broadcasting revenue for the club fell 4.5%, primarily due to the men’s first team participating in the UEFA Europa League in the prior year quarter, with no UEFA competition in the current year quarter. Commercial and matchday revenues fell 1.3% and 1.1%, respectively.

For the three months ended September 30, the club reported a loss per share of 3.85p, compared to a profit of 0.78p per share for the same period a year earlier.

On an adjusted basis, it lost 1.48p per share, compared to a loss of 0.28p per share last year.

Total revenue fell 2% to £140.3M during the quarter and was below the FactSet consensus estimate of £140.6M; adjusted EBITDA rose 13.5% to £26.9M.

For the full year, Manchester United continues to expect revenue between £640M and £660M (mid. £650M; est. £655M) and adjusted EBITDA of £180M to £200M.

data.title: Oracle post-earnings slide weighs on S&P, Nasdaq 100 futures
data.description:
Benchmark S&P 500 futures and tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 futures were under pressure on Thursday after Oracle (ORCL) posted mixed results, pressuring the AI trade.

S&P futures (SPX) slipped -0.25% to 6,869.36 points, and Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) declined -0.35% to 25,686.62 points. Dow futures (INDU) edged higher by +0.09% to 48,102.86 points. 

“Bond yields have also moved lower, which partly reflects the Fed and the wider risk-off tone this morning,” stated Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid. He noted Japan’s robust bond auction and Australia’s unexpected employment contraction as contributing factors.

Yields were lower across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.14%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 1.3 basis points to 3.54%. The longer-end 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) dipped slightly by -0.08% to 4.78%. Short-term rates also declined, with the 3-month yield (US3M) decreasing by 1.5 basis points to 3.67%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included Nordson (NDSN) +4.52%, Arista Networks (ANET) +2.77%, and DTE Energy (DTE) +1.48%. Notable decliners were led by Oracle (ORCL) with a sharp -12.83% drop, followed by International Flavors & Fragrances (IFF) -2.97% and Coinbase Global (COIN) -2.20%.

data.title: Lovesac's Q3 results fall short of expectations, cuts FY26 guidance on soft Q3 outcome
data.description:
Weighed down by increased transportation and tariff costs, and with sales only up fractionally from a year ago, Lovesac (LOVE) reported a wider-than-expected loss in the third quarter, and lowered its full year guidance below estimates as the company continues to struggle with a challenging macro environment for the furniture business.

Shares are down more than 15% in Thursday’s premarket trading and are expected to open near its 5-year low of $11.26.

“Lovesac (LOVE) is inventing and investing steadily, even through these tough times for our category, while balancing cash flow generation and profitability,” said CEO Shawn Nelson. “We are totally focused and committed to this goal that we believe can produce meaningful growth over the next few years—regardless of what happens in the macro environment.”

With a 13% gain in showroom sales partially offsetting a 17% drop in internet sales, total net sales increased by just 0.2% to $150.2M, falling short of the consensus estimate by $4M.

Additionally, as net expenses were up in additional categories like advertising and marketing, Lovesac (LOVE) fell further into the red with a loss of $0.72 per share versus a loss of $0.32 a year ago and 3 cents worse than anticipated, Additionally, a key operating income tflipped negative with adjusted EBITDA negative $6M from a gain of $2.7M a year ago.

The result of which led to a compressed gross profit margin of 56.1%, down 240 basis points year-over-year.

For the current quarter, Lovesac (LOVE) expects to report a profit of $1.88 to $2.22 per share on sales between $236M and $256M, both of which are below estimates of $2.33 and $260.74, respectively, but includes sales from Black Friday and Cyber Monday.  

For FY26, the company now expects a profit of $0.15 to $0.49 per share from earlier guidance of $0.52 to $1.05 per share versus the consensus estimate of $0.68.

The company’s sales outlook was also lowered to a range of $685M and $705M from $710M and $740M, with the $695M midpoint of the new range less than $713.56M estimates.

data.title: ZenaTech signs offer to acquire surveying firm in US
data.description:
• ZenaTech (ZENA) on Thursday said it would acquire a surveying firm headquartered in the Mountain West/Rocky Mountain region of the US with existing licensing to operate in multiple states.
  • "The potential acquisition enhances ZenaTech’s presence in the region and capacity to provide drone-enabled advanced land surveying as well as drone services for modern crop and livestock management, wildfire management, and land stewardship," the company said.
  • ZENA -0.61% premarket to $3.24.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AC Immune surges on positive mid-stage trial data for Parkinson’s drug
data.description:
AC Immune (ACIU) added ~30% in the premarket on Thursday after the Swiss biotech announced positive initial results from a mid-stage trial for ACI-7104.056, an immunotherapy targeting early Parkinson’s disease.

Based on interim data from Part 1 of its Phase 2 VacSYn trial, which enrolled 34 patients with early PD, the company said that the placebo-controlled study met all target criteria for immunogenicity.

According to ACIU, all trial subjects included in the analysis had received at least 12 months (48 weeks) of ACI-7104.056, which is designed to act on alpha-synuclein (a-syn), a pathology causing PD.

The experimental therapy was generally safe and well-tolerated, the company said, citing data at weeks 50 and 76.

AC Immune (ACIU) added that the readout demonstrated for the first time that targeting a-syn pathology with active immunotherapy could slow the rate of PD progression.

The company expects to meet with regulatory authorities to obtain feedback regarding its plans to accelerate the drug's development towards registration. Final data from Part 1 of the study are anticipated in mid-2026.

data.title: Diamond Hill stock jumps on ~$473M acquisition by First Eagle
data.description:
Diamond Hill (DHIL) stock was surging after the boutique investment management firm announced an ~$473M acquisition by First Eagle Investments.

Shares were +42.58% Thursday pre-market to $167.50.

Under the terms of the transaction, First Eagle Investments is set to pay $175 per share in cash. The purchase price represents a 49% premium over Diamond Hill’s closing share price of $117.48 on December 10.

The acquisition cost represents a 44% premium over the company's 30-day volume-weighted average price.

The deal is expected to increase First Eagle's footprint in traditional fixed income.

Diamond Hill said it will maintain its headquarters in Columbus and operate under its existing brand. As part of the agreement, the target company will not pay quarterly dividends through closing.

Pro forma for the transaction, First Eagle's assets under management and assets under advisement are expected to be ~$208B as of September 30.

The transaction is expected to close by the third quarter of 2026.
